title: Promotion path: KERNEL-003 ValidationRunner -> KERNEL-001 PromotionGate over SandboxAdapter handle
goal: Wire the sandbox-routed ValidationRunner (MT-050) into the existing KERNEL-001 PromotionGate so that successful sandbox-executed validations promote artifacts to authority. Preserves KERNEL-003 promotion semantics; the new sandbox boundary becomes the trust line. ProcessHandle from the SandboxAdapter is the durable receipt that promotion writes into the EventLedger for replay (HBR-INT-001).
expected_diff_shape: New src/backend/handshake_core/src/sandbox/promotion_binding.rs that wraps a ValidationRunner result + ProcessHandle into a PromotionRequest; edits to the KERNEL-001 PromotionGate module (locate via Grep) accepting a new PromotionRequest variant carrying ProcessHandle as evidence. Tests under src/backend/handshake_core/tests/sandbox_promotion_binding_tests.rs.
unblock_conditions: MT-050 ValidationRunner binding merged; KERNEL-001 PromotionGate already in main (verify path).
implementation_notes: Pre-step: Grep `pub struct PromotionGate` and `pub enum PromotionRequest` in src/backend/handshake_core/src/kernel/** to locate the KERNEL-001 surface and the existing request variants. promotion_binding.rs: `pub struct SandboxValidationEvidence { process_handle: ProcessHandle, adapter_capabilities: AdapterCapabilities, validation_exit_code: i32, validation_stdout_artifact_id: ArtifactId, validation_stderr_artifact_id: ArtifactId, sandbox_runtime_ms: u64 }` and `pub fn build_promotion_request(evidence: SandboxValidationEvidence, candidate_artifact: ArtifactId) -> PromotionRequest`. The PromotionGate accepts this new request shape; on success, the gate writes an EventLedger row of family `FR-EVT-PROMOTION-SANDBOX-VALIDATED` carrying the ProcessHandle.id (UUID v7) so HBR-INT-001 replay can reconstruct which adapter+handle produced the validated artifact. Critically: the PromotionGate REJECTS evidence where adapter_capabilities.filesystem_isolation_strength==Weak OR network_isolation_strength==Weak — sandbox quality is part of the promotion criterion (prevents an attacker swapping in a fake/no-op adapter to bypass validation). Add `pub enum PromotionRejectReason { ... InsufficientSandboxIsolation { required_min: IsolationStrength, observed: IsolationStrength }, ... }`. Tests: (a) successful promotion writes the FR-EVT-PROMOTION-SANDBOX-VALIDATED row containing the ProcessHandle.id; (b) Weak isolation rejects with InsufficientSandboxIsolation; (c) non-zero validation_exit_code rejects with the existing KERNEL-001 reject variant (preserve behavior); (d) replay-equivalence test: given two recorded EventLedger streams that differ only in ProcessHandle.id values, both produce identical projected promotion state (HBR-INT-001 replay invariant).
risk_if_missed: Without binding, sandbox-validated artifacts cannot reach PromotionGate authority; the KERNEL-003 promotion path silently bypasses the new sandbox isolation guarantee.
validator_focus: (a) Weak isolation rejection works; (b) EventLedger row carries ProcessHandle.id (UUID v7); (c) replay-equivalence test passes; (d) existing KERNEL-001 PromotionGate reject variants are preserved (no regression).

minimum_controls: Weak isolation rejection prevents an attacker (or accidental NoopAdapter wiring) from promoting unsafe artifacts.
Replay-equivalence test prevents promotion state from becoming sandbox-internal-id-dependent (would break HBR-INT-001 EventLedger replay).
Preserve existing KERNEL-001 PromotionGate variants; this MT is additive.
